content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

June 29, 1995

Shelley Mateo
6110 Cobblestone Road
Placerville, CA 95667

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-197

Dear Ms. Mateo:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding your responsibilities as a designated employee of the Department of Finance under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Must you report free transportation and lodging provided by the Golden One Credit Union in relation to an educational conference which was provided to your spouse if you accompany your spouse on the trip?

CONCLUSION

So long as the Golden One Credit Union does not specifically request your attendance or otherwise control the payment to you,

_________________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18995. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-95-197
Page 2


the payment for travel and accommodations related to the
educational conference provided to your spouse as part of his
employment relationship with the Golden One Credit Union will not
be considered a gift to you.

FACTS

You are employed by the Department of Finance. Your spouse
is a volunteer board member for the Golden One Credit Union.
Periodically, the Golden One Credit Union provides transportation
and lodging for board members to educational conferences. The
credit union permits board members to take their spouses. You
have asked whether you receive a disclosable gift under the Act if
you accompany your spouse on the trip.

ANALYSIS

Regulation 18944 provides:

(a) Gifts given directly to members of an
official's immediate family are not gifts to the
official unless used or disposed of by the official
or given by the recipient member of the official's
immediate family to the official for disposition or
use at the official's discretion.

(b) Gifts delivered by mail or other written
communication are given directly to members of the
official's immediate family if the family members'
names or familial designations (such as "spouse")
appear in the address on the envelope or in the
communication tendering or offering the gift, and
the gift is intended for their use or enjoyment.

(c) A gift given to the official, but
designated for the official and spouse or family,
is a gift to the official if the official exercises
discretion and control over who will actually use
the gift.

(d) If the official enjoys direct benefit
from a single gift, as well as members of the
official's family, the full value of the gift is
attributable to the official.

Pursuant to Regulation 18944(d), when a gift is received by
the spouse or immediate family member of an official, and the
official enjoys a direct benefit from the gift, the gift will be
deemed to have been received by the official.

However, under your facts, the payment for travel and
accommodations related to the educational conference was provided
to your spouse as part of his employment relationship with the
Golden One Credit Union as a volunteer board member. You stated

Our File No. A-95-197
Page 3


that the donor was interested only in the board member's
attendance, but permitted him to bring his spouse.

The Commission has consistently advised that where the
circumstances surrounding the payment clearly indicate that it was
intended for the spouse of the official (and not the official),
and the official incidentally enjoys a benefit from the payment,
the official will not be deemed to have received a gift. (Combs
Advice Letter, No. A-87-141; Sutton Advice Letter, No. A-91-094
and Brown Advice Letter, No. I-90-602.) Under such circumstances,
the benefit the official receives is from the official's spouse
and no reportable gift results. (Section 82028(b)(3).)2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.3

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division


2 Please note that if the Golden One Credit Union specifically
requested your attendance or otherwise controlled the payment to
you, this analysis would not apply.

3 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.
